perturbation.original_text: 1. License - Individual grants Lifeway together with its affiliates, subsidiaries, parent companies and their representatives and employees have an unlimited, perpetual, non-exclusive, worldwide and, except as set forth in Section 9, royalty-free, right to use, reuse, publish, reproduce, perform, copy, create derivative works, exhibit, broadcast, and display throughout the world the name, image and likeness of Individual in Marketing Materials (as defined below) in connection with marketing, advertising or otherwise promoting the Lifeway products and/or services and for historical reference and display purposes and other internal purposes, including without limitation, internal sales meetings. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, Lifeway shall be under no obligation to cause the Marketing Materials to be displayed, published or performed. Individual further waives the right to inspect or approve the Marketing Materials.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. License - Individual grants Lifeway together with its affiliates, subsidiaries, parent companies and their representatives and employees have an unlimited, terminable at will by the Individual, non-exclusive, worldwide and, except as set forth in Section 9, royalty-free, right to use, reuse, publish, reproduce, perform, copy, create derivative works, exhibit, broadcast, and display throughout the world the name, image and likeness of Individual in Marketing Materials (as defined below) in connection with marketing, advertising or otherwise promoting the Lifeway products and/or services and for historical reference and display purposes and other internal purposes, including without limitation, internal sales meetings. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, Lifeway shall be under no obligation to cause the Marketing Materials to be displayed, published or performed. Individual further waives the right to inspect or approve the Marketing Materials.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'perpetual' to 'terminable at will' creates a direct contradiction. A perpetual license, by definition, continues indefinitely unless otherwise specified. A license terminable at will can be revoked at any time by the grantor. This introduces uncertainty regarding the duration and enforceability of the license granted to Lifeway.
perturbation.law_explanation: Under Illinois law, consideration is a required element for the creation of a valid and enforceable contract. If the license granted to Lifeway is 'terminable at will' by the Individual, the consideration may be deemed illusory, especially if Lifeway expended significant resources relying on the 'perpetual' license initially granted. This could render the contract unenforceable due to lack of valid consideration.

perturbation.justification: The change from 'perpetual' to 'terminable at will' directly contradicts the nature of a perpetual license. The Law Explanation suggests this could render the contract unenforceable due to a lack of valid consideration, as the benefit to Lifeway is uncertain. The provided snippet states that the Illinois Compiled Statutes is maintained for legislative drafting purposes. The change creates a contradiction in terms, as a perpetual license cannot be terminated at will by the individual, thus undermining the initial agreement and potentially making the consideration illusory.

perturbation.original_text: Individual represents and warrants that any testimonial or review provided reflects her own honest opinions, findings, beliefs, or experiences. Individual represents that she is a bona fide user of Lifeway products and/or services.
perturbation.changed_text: Individual represents and warrants that any testimonial or review provided reflects opinions, findings, beliefs, or experiences that Individual has pre-approved with Lifeway. Individual does not represent that she is a bona fide user of Lifeway products and/or services.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying the representation about the testimonial's honesty and bona fide user status introduces a potential contradiction with advertising regulations concerning endorsements and testimonials. By stating the review has been 'pre-approved' by Lifeway and that the individual may not actually be a 'bona fide user,' the contract implies that the endorsement may be misleading or deceptive.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 5 of the FTC Act prohibits unfair or deceptive acts or practices in commerce. Endorsements must reflect the honest opinions, findings, beliefs, or experiences of the endorser. If the endorsement is pre-approved by Lifeway and the endorser is not a bona fide user, it may be considered a deceptive practice violating the FTC Act.

Under this Act, as amended, the Commission is empowered, among other things, to (a) prevent unfair methods of competition and unfair or deceptive acts or practices in or affecting commerce; (b) seek monetary redress and other relief for conduct injurious to consumers; (c) prescribe rules defining with specificity acts or practices that are unfair or deceptive, and establishing requirements designed to prevent such acts or practices; (d) gather and compile information and conduct investigations relating to the organization, business, practices, and management of entities engaged in commerce; and (e) make reports and legislative recommendations to Congress and the public. A number of other statutes listed here are enforced under the FTC Act. Return to top

perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts Section 5 of the FTC Act, which prohibits unfair or deceptive acts or practices. By stating that the endorsement has been 'pre-approved' by Lifeway and that the individual may not actually be a 'bona fide user,' the changed text implies that the endorsement may be misleading or deceptive. This directly conflicts with the requirement that endorsements reflect the honest opinions of the endorser as described in the Law Explanation, violating the FTC Act as described in Scraped Law Snippet 1: 'prevent unfair methods of competition and unfair or deceptive acts or practices in or affecting commerce'.

perturbation.original_text: All undisputed Royalty payments shall be made in U.S. dollars in cash or to the order of Individual and shall be due and payable within thirty (30) days after the end of each calendar month for sales during the previous month.
perturbation.changed_text: All Royalty payments shall be made in U.S. dollars or cryptocurrency at Lifeway's sole discretion and shall be due and payable based on Lifeway's cashflow availability.
perturbation.explanation: This modification alters the method and timing of royalty payments. Replacing 'cash' with 'cryptocurrency at Lifeway's sole discretion' and changing the payment schedule to 'Lifeway's cashflow availability' introduces ambiguity and potential non-compliance with contractual obligations regarding payment terms.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Uniform Commercial Code § 2-311 (Options and Cooperation Respecting Performance)
perturbation.law_explanation: UCC § 2-311 states that 'an agreement for sale which is otherwise sufficiently definite to be a contract is not made invalid by the fact that it leaves particulars of performance to be specified by one of the parties.' However, this specification must be made in good faith and within limits set by commercial reasonableness. Allowing Lifeway to pay in cryptocurrency at its sole discretion, and delaying payments based on cashflow availability, could be interpreted as a lack of good faith, making the agreement's payment terms indefinite and potentially unenforceable under the UCC, especially in states (like Illinois, the governing law) that have adopted it.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-311. Options and Cooperation Respecting Performance. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-311. Options and Cooperation Respecting Performance. (1) An agreement for sale which is otherwise sufficiently definite (subsection (3) of Section 2-204 ) to be a contract is not made invalid by the fact that it leaves particulars of performance to be specified by one of the parties. Any such specification must be made in good faith and within limits set by commercial reasonableness. (2) Unless otherwise agreed specifications relating to assortment of the goods are at the buyer's option and except as otherwise provided in subsections (1)(c) and (3) of Section 2-319 specifications or arrangements relating to shipment are at the seller's option. (3)Where such specification would materially affect the other party's performance but is not seasonably made or where one party's cooperation is necessary to the agreed performance of the other but is not seasonably forthcoming, the other party in addition to all other remedies (a) is excused for any resulting delay in his own performance; and (b) may also either proceed to perform in any reasonable manner or after the time for a material part of his own performance treat the failure to specify or to cooperate as a breach by failure to deliver or accept the goods . Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a legal contradiction under the category of Misaligned Terminology - Legal Contradiction. The original contract specified payment in 'cash,' aligning with standard commercial practices and legal interpretations. The changed text introduces 'cryptocurrency at Lifeway's sole discretion' and alters the payment schedule based on 'Lifeway's cashflow availability.' This deviates from legally recognized definitions and standards for payment terms, potentially making the contract unenforceable. Scraped Law Snippet 1, referring to UCC § 2-311, emphasizes that specifications of performance must be made in good faith and within limits set by commercial reasonableness. The change to cryptocurrency payment at Lifeway's discretion and alteration of the payment schedule potentially violates this requirement, creating a contradiction by deviating from commercially reasonable payment terms and introducing ambiguity and uncertainty in payment.
